title: Travel Smart, Travel Safe: Preparing Your Vehicle for the Festive Season
description: LIFESTYLE NEWS - As South Africans prepare...
bodyText:
LIFESTYLE NEWS - As South Africans prepare for their annual festive season road trips, the importance of road safety once again takes centre stage.


> The year-end holidays are traditionally one of the busiest times on the country’s roads and, unfortunately, also one of the most dangerous.


Between 1 and 19 December 2024, Gauteng alone recorded 96 fatalities from 90 fatal crashes.

With rising travel costs, many families are choosing to drive to their destinations.

This makes it more important than ever to ensure that vehicles are roadworthy and ready for the journey ahead.


## Why vehicle safety checks matter


South Africa records thousands of road fatalities each year. Mechanical failure and poorly maintained vehicles play a significant role in many of these accidents.

A pre-trip inspection is more than a precaution; it is a responsibility to yourself, your passengers, and every road user you encounter.

Ashley Reddy, Head of Aftersales and Dealer Network Management at Audi South Africa, says:“Many road users underestimate how small issues, such as worn tyres or low brake fluid, can snowball into serious dangers at high speed. A 20-minute check before a trip could be the difference between a safe arrival and a life-changing incident.”


## What to check before a long drive


Experts recommend focusing on the basics before setting off:

- Tyres and brakes: Ensure tyres have adequate tread and are inflated to the correct pressure. Brake pads should be inspected for wear.
- Lights and wipers: Visibility is critical. Test headlights, brake lights, indicators, and wipers.
- Fluids: Check engine oil, coolant, and windscreen washer fluid.
- Spare equipment: Confirm that your spare wheel, jack, and tools are in good condition.

These steps may sound simple, but collectively they reduce the likelihood of accidents significantly.

Markus Schuster, Head of Audi South Africa, adds:“Planning a journey is not only about the route and destination; it is about ensuring your vehicle is prepared to get you there safely. In South Africa’s festive season, when road traffic is at its peak, being proactive about safety is a civic duty as much as it is personal care.”


## Beyond the Vehicle: Road Safety Habits


Vehicle checks are only part of the equation. Safe driving behaviour is equally vital:

- Stick to speed limits and adjust for weather conditions.
- Avoid fatigue by resting every two hours.
- Never drive under the influence.
- Share the road responsibly with cyclists, motorcyclists, and pedestrians.

As road safety authorities remind us each festive season, reducing fatalities requires shared responsibility.


## Safe travels


While every driver can and should carry out basic checks themselves, a professional inspection offers added peace of mind.

For example, Audi offers a complimentary 20-point Health Check through its service network. These checks cover critical areas such as tyres, brakes, lights, wipers and fluid levels, helping to keep vehicles roadworthy and safe.

Audi’s commitment to safety extends beyond maintenance. Many models in the Audi range offer optional advanced driver assistance systems, designed to enhance awareness and support safer driving.

In addition, every new Audi comes standard with a comprehensive five-year or 100 000 km Freeway Plan, with the option to extend this cover for up to 15 years or 300 000 km.

Recognised as one of the best in class, it offers peace of mind and ongoing support long after leaving the showroom.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
